zh.Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, England: 英國賀福爾布藍普頓 布萊恩
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edmund de Cornwall於大約1284年出生在英國史洛普爾金萊特時，他的父親Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock33歲，母親Joan FitzAlan26歲。他於1312年與Elizabeth Brampton在英國賀福爾布藍普頓 布萊恩結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1354年3月22日在他的家鄉享年71歲。

zh-hans.Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, England: 英国賀福爾布藍普頓 布萊恩
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edmund de Cornwall于大约1284年出生在英国史洛普爾金萊特时，他的父亲Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock33岁，母亲Joan FitzAlan26岁。他于1312年与Elizabeth Brampton在英国賀福爾布藍普頓 布萊恩结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1354年3月22日在他的家乡享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edmund de Cornwall was born about 1284, in Kinlet, Shropshire, England, his father, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, was 33 and his mother, Joan FitzAlan, was 26. He married Elizabeth Brampton in 1312, in Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 22 March 1354, in his hometown, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edmund de Cornwall est né environ 1284, à Kinlet, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan FitzAlan, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Brampton en 1312, à Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 22 mars 1354, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edmund de Cornwall um 1284 in Kinlet, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock 33 und seine Mutter Joan FitzAlan 26. Er hat 1312 in Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, England Elizabeth Brampton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 22. März 1354 im Alter von 71 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund de Cornwall è nato il circa 1284 nel Kinlet, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Joan FitzAlan, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Brampton nel 1312, nel Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 22 marzo 1354, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund de Cornwallが約1284年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアキンレットで生まれた時，父Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnockは33歳，母Joan FitzAlanは26歳であった。1312年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブランプトン ブリアンでElizabeth Bramptonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1354年3月22日に故郷で71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund de Cornwall(이)가 대략 1284년 영국 슈럽실르 킨렛에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan FitzAlan(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1312년 영국 허퍼드실르 브램프턴 브라이언에서 Elizabeth Brampton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1354년 3월 22일 그의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund de Cornwall nasceu aproximadamente 1284, em Kinlet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Joan FitzAlan, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Brampton em 1312, em Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 22 de março de 1354, em sua cidade natal, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edmund de Cornwall родился около 1284 г. в местности кинлет, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan FitzAlan, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Brampton в 1312 г. в местности брамптон брян, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 22 марта 1354 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edmund de Cornwall nació aproximadamente 1284, en Kinlet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Joan FitzAlan, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Brampton en 1312, en Brampton Bryan, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 22 de marzo de 1354, en su pueblo, a la edad de 71 años.
